title: Inert attribute pattern to disable parts of UI when modal opens, without losing layout
scenario_context: When modal opens, background should be inert (not focusable/clickable). inert attribute works in modern browsers; aria-hidden + tabindex=-1 fallback otherwise.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Removing background from DOM (loses scroll position)
responsive_reasoning: 1. inert is modern, native solution.\n2. aria-hidden + tabindex management as fallback.\n3. Works across all viewports.

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Click background doesn't activate (unless dismissal pattern)

performance_notes: inert is native — no JS for compliant browsers. Fallback only mutates DOM on transitions.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-hidden tells screen readers. inert prevents focus traversal.
edge_cases_handled: Modal close — inert removed, focus returns
what_breaks_naive_approach: Without inert, Tab can escape modal into background. aria-hidden alone doesn't block focus.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Native inert + fallback. 2) Focus management free. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Card grid with cards that grow to fill remaining row when fewer cards
viewport_spectrum: Card layouts with variable item counts
scenario_context: 5 cards in a row that auto-fits 3 per row. Last row has 2 cards — they should expand to fill the row evenly, not be left with empty space.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Last row cards sized like full rows (gap to right)
responsive_reasoning: 1. auto-fit with 1fr automatically distributes remaining width.\n2. Works elegantly with any item count.
accessibility_across_viewports: DOM order respected.
edge_cases_handled: 3 cards, container fits 5 — each card is 1/3 wide (auto-fit fills)
5 cards, container fits 3 — first row 3 cards, second row 2 cards at 1/2 each
what_breaks_naive_approach: auto-fill leaves last row cards at min width with empty space. auto-fit is what most apps want.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) auto-fit vs auto-fill choice. 2) Cards adapt to any count. 3) Pure CSS.

title: Stat counter with fluid digit width that doesn't jump as numbers change
scenario_context: Animated counter 1 → 1,234,567. Digit widths vary in proportional fonts causing layout shift as digits change. tabular-nums + fluid type solves.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Fixed font size that breaks at narrow viewports
responsive_reasoning: 1. tabular-nums = monospace digits.\n2. Fluid font size via clamp.\n3. Animation smooth without layout jump.
performance_notes: rAF animation smooth. tabular-nums prevents reflow per digit.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-live announces final value (polite avoids interruption).
edge_cases_handled: Counter changes mid-animation — restarts from current value
Very large numbers — tabular-nums prevents jitter
Reduced motion — could snap to final value
what_breaks_naive_approach: Proportional digits cause counter to wiggle as numbers change. Fixed font size breaks on narrow.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) tabular-nums for stability. 2) Fluid clamp size. 3) Locale-aware formatting.

title: Sidebar collapsible to icons only with smooth width transition
viewport_spectrum: Desktop sidebar that user can toggle compact
scenario_context: Sidebar that user can collapse to icon-only rail (64px) and expand to full (240px). Smooth transition; container queries inside adapt children.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Children that don't adapt to compact state
responsive_reasoning: 1. Sidebar width is user-controlled.\n2. Container queries on sidebar drive child layout.\n3. Smooth transition preserves context.
performance_notes: CSS transition GPU. Container queries efficient.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-expanded on toggle. title attribute on links shows label on hover when collapsed.
edge_cases_handled: Collapsed — only icons visible, title on hover
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hiding labels with display:none kills layout context. CSS transition without container query means children don't adapt.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Smooth transition. 2) Container-aware children. 3) Keyboard shortcut + persistence.

title: Carousel with mouse drag and touch swipe unified via pointer events
scenario_context: Image carousel. Touch users swipe. Desktop users want to drag with mouse. Pointer events unify; momentum on release.
responsive_reasoning: 1. PointerEvent unifies mouse + touch + pen.\n2. Drag tracking + velocity for momentum.\n3. Snap to nearest slide on release.

layout_strategy.content_priority: Slides accessible to all input methods

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: Click + drag to scrub through slides

performance_notes: Native scroll-snap + JS for drag. setPointerCapture ensures consistent drag tracking.
accessibility_across_viewports: Dots have aria-label + aria-current. Arrow keys via native scroll. Touch-action pan-y allows vertical scroll outside.
edge_cases_handled: Touch swipe — native scroll-snap + JS drag combined
what_breaks_naive_approach: Touch-only carousel misses desktop drag UX. No snap is jittery.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pointer events for all input. 2) Snap for clean stops. 3) Dots for tap navigation.

title: Form with submission button that stays visible above keyboard even on extreme overflow
scenario_context: Sign-up form with 15 fields, scrollable. Submit button at bottom. When keyboard appears, button must remain accessible — sticky positioned to keyboard inset.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Submit button at form bottom only (hidden by keyboard)
Sticky submit that doesn't account for keyboard
responsive_reasoning: 1. Submit must be reachable while user fills form.\n2. Sticky bottom with keyboard inset translation.\n3. Padding compensates so last field visible.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Fill fields, tap Submit above keyboard

performance_notes: Single keyboard listener shared globally. Transform GPU.
accessibility_across_viewports: Real form. Submit button always accessible via Tab + Enter.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Submit at static bottom is behind keyboard.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Keyboard-aware sticky. 2) Scroll padding compensates. 3) Smooth transitions.

title: Snackbar/toast position that respects bottom nav and home indicator on mobile
viewport_spectrum: Mobile with bottom nav + home indicator
scenario_context: Snackbar message. Mobile with bottom nav: must appear above nav + home indicator. Desktop: no such concern.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Fixed pixel offsets that don't adapt
responsive_reasoning: 1. Snackbar must be above bottom nav + safe area.\n2. CSS variable for bottom nav height.\n3. env() for home indicator.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Stacking bottom offsets via CSS vars

accessibility_across_viewports: role=status aria-live=polite. Dismiss button 36pt (could be 44pt).
edge_cases_handled: With bottom nav + indicator — stacks correctly
Without bottom nav — uses just safe area
Desktop — no bottom adornments, baseline padding
Multiple stacked snackbars — could add to array (production)
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hardcoded bottom: 1rem on mobile is behind bottom nav.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) CSS vars stack additively. 2) Safe area aware. 3) Universal placement logic.

title: Story-style vertical content viewer (Instagram Stories) that adapts to viewport orientation
viewport_spectrum: Phone portrait (full screen story), landscape (centered with side bars)
scenario_context: Vertical story viewer. Portrait: fills screen. Landscape: video stays 9:16 aspect, centered with black side bars (letterboxed).
responsive_reasoning: 1. 9:16 content is inherently portrait.\n2. Landscape letterboxes; controls move to safe positions.\n3. Same content, different containment.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap right/left to advance/back, hold to pause

accessibility_across_viewports: Story video has controls. Aria-label per progress bar.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Filling viewport in landscape stretches story horizontally — content broken.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Aspect-ratio preserves story integrity. 2) Letterbox in landscape. 3) Safe area aware.

title: iPad-specific multitasking awareness: handle width changes from Split View / Slide Over
scenario_context: iPad user can drag another app to create Split View — your app suddenly resizes from 1180px to 320-694px. Layout must adapt smoothly without losing state.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Layout that fails at unexpected widths
responsive_reasoning: 1. Width changes can be drastic (1180 → 320).\n2. responsive-001 hook handles this naturally.\n3. State lives outside layout components.
accessibility_across_viewports: Same a11y across layouts.
edge_cases_handled: Drag app to Split View — width drops, layout swaps
Mid-edit when resized — editing state preserved
Drag away from Split View — full width restored
Slide Over — even narrower, same logic
what_breaks_naive_approach: State in layout component is lost on layout swap.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) State outside layout. 2) Width-driven adaptation. 3) iPad multitasking 'just works'.

title: Notion-style page with nested sub-pages and tabs that all collapse on mobile
scenario_context: Page with sub-pages (nested) and tabs. Desktop: nested tabs visible. Mobile: tabs become bottom-sheet picker.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Hierarchical content needs hierarchical UI.\n2. Mobile collapses tab UI into picker.\n3. Same data; navigation chrome differs.

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Mobile shows only current tab name

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap tab name → bottom sheet picker

performance_notes: Mobile picker only mounts on tap. Tab content lazy if needed.
accessibility_across_viewports: tablist/tab on desktop. role=dialog on mobile picker. aria-current=page on selected.
edge_cases_handled: Long tab labels — mobile shows full in picker
Resize from desktop to mobile — current tab preserved
what_breaks_naive_approach: Many tabs cramped on mobile. Tab bar wraps to multiple lines unreadable.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Picker vs tabs per viewport. 2) Same data. 3) Same active state preserved.

title: Sortable table headers that show direction with adaptive icon size
scenario_context: Click column header to sort. Show arrow indicating direction. Icon size adapts to viewport (smaller mobile, larger desktop).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Tiny icon hard to see on mobile
responsive_reasoning: 1. Sort indication is visual + a11y.\n2. Adaptive sizing via cqi or text size.\n3. aria-sort for screen readers.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Real button in th with arrow icon

interaction_model_per_device.desktop_keyboard: Tab to header, Enter to sort

accessibility_across_viewports: aria-sort is the canonical accessibility attribute. Real button is keyboard accessible. Icons aria-hidden (state in aria-sort).
what_breaks_naive_approach: div-with-onClick is not keyboard accessible. No aria-sort = screen readers can't communicate state.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Native semantics. 2) Universal accessibility. 3) Clear visual + a11y state.

title: Checkbox group with adaptive layout: column on narrow, grid on wide
scenario_context: Filter checkboxes (categories). Narrow: vertical list. Wide: 2-3 column grid for compactness.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Checkboxes are independent — grid is fine.\n2. Auto-fit grid for natural adaptation.\n3. 44pt tap targets.
accessibility_across_viewports: fieldset/legend for grouping. Real checkbox inputs. 44pt label area.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Fixed grid wastes/crowds depending on viewport.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Auto-fit grid. 2) Native semantics. 3) Tap target compliance.

title: Video that respects user's prefers-reduced-motion and autoplay only when allowed
scenario_context: Background video that autoplays. Should respect prefers-reduced-motion — pause for those users.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Hidden controls so user can't pause
responsive_reasoning: 1. Autoplay is opt-in for accessibility.\n2. Respect motion preference.\n3. Controls always available.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap to play if not auto

accessibility_across_viewports: aria-label. controls=true for reduced-motion users.
edge_cases_handled: Reduced motion ON — controls shown, no autoplay
Reduced motion OFF — autoplay loop muted
what_breaks_naive_approach: Forced autoplay is accessibility hostile.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Motion preference respected. 2) Controls for opt-out users. 3) Standard video element.

title: Anchor link navigation in long article with sticky highlighted current section
scenario_context: TOC highlights current section as user scrolls. IntersectionObserver determines visible section.
responsive_reasoning: 1. IntersectionObserver is efficient.\n2. First visible section is 'current'.\n3. Smooth scroll on TOC click.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-current marks current section. Real anchor links work with browser history.
edge_cases_handled: Section taller than viewport — first visible wins
Bottom of article — last section stays highlighted
Scroll-snap or smooth scroll — IO still fires correctly
what_breaks_naive_approach: Manual scroll listener is laggy. Without IO, syncing with scroll position is buggy.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) IntersectionObserver is efficient. 2) DOM-order priority for visible section. 3) Anchor links + back button work.

title: Backdrop dismiss vs explicit close: configurable per modal
scenario_context: Some modals should NOT close on backdrop click (e.g., critical confirmation, in-progress form). Configurable behavior.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: All modals dismiss on backdrop (dangerous for forms)
No way to escape modal (terrible UX)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Critical modals shouldn't close from accidental tap.\n2. Always have explicit close (X button + Escape).\n3. Configurable backdrop behavior.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap backdrop OR X to close

accessibility_across_viewports: role=dialog aria-modal. Always-explicit close button. Escape default but configurable for critical modals where you want only intentional close.
edge_cases_handled: Critical confirm — dismissOnBackdrop=false prevents accidental close
what_breaks_naive_approach: Always-dismissible dangerous for forms (one tap loses data). No-way-to-dismiss frustrating.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Configurable behavior. 2) Always-explicit close button. 3) Universal pattern.

title: Very low-end Android (1GB RAM, slow CPU) — minimize JS bundle and DOM size
scenario_context: Apps must work on $50 Android phones with 1GB RAM. Heavy bundles crash or freeze. Strategy: defer everything not critical, minimize DOM, use CSS over JS.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: 500KB JS bundle (parse takes 5s on slow CPU)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Bundle size and DOM size matter more than viewport.\n2. Lazy load non-critical JS.\n3. CSS where possible.
performance_notes: Initial JS <50KB. Defer everything else. CSS-only animations.
accessibility_across_viewports: Server-rendered HTML works without JS — assistive tech sees content immediately.
edge_cases_handled: No JS available — core HTML works
Out of memory — fewer DOM nodes
what_breaks_naive_approach: 500KB initial bundle delays LCP 5+ seconds on slow CPU. Crashes possible on 1GB RAM.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Aggressive code splitting. 2) Server-first. 3) Network/CPU aware loading.

title: Hook-based detection of viewport changes without re-renders for non-React consumers
viewport_spectrum: Any with viewport-aware non-React code (e.g., canvas, three.js)
scenario_context: Canvas-based visualization needs viewport size but shouldn't re-render React tree. Use event emitter pattern.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: React state for canvas-only viewport (causes re-renders)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Some consumers don't need React re-render.\n2. Event emitter or context with refs.\n3. Canvas resizes itself.
performance_notes: No React state = no re-renders. Direct canvas mutation.
accessibility_across_viewports: Canvas should have role=img + aria-label for any meaningful content.
edge_cases_handled: Heavy canvas — no React thrash on resize
Window resize — single listener fires all
what_breaks_naive_approach: React state for viewport on canvas component causes full re-render of expensive draw logic.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) No-re-render hook. 2) Imperative canvas updates. 3) Single listener pattern.

title: Form field that switches between horizontal label+input and stacked based on container width
viewport_spectrum: Forms in narrow modals vs wide pages
scenario_context: Form field. Wide container: label left, input right (horizontal). Narrow: label above input (stacked).
responsive_reasoning: 1. Container width determines whether horizontal layout fits.\n2. Container query switches.\n3. Same component.
accessibility_across_viewports: Real label/input pair. htmlFor connects.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Horizontal fields cramped in narrow modals.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Container-aware label position. 2) Same component everywhere. 3) Consistent alignment.

title: Article hero with title that auto-fits to viewport width using fluid clamp + balance
scenario_context: Hero title 'Discover the future of work' should be largest readable size at any viewport without overflow.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Fixed font size that overflows narrow
responsive_reasoning: 1. clamp combines minimum, fluid middle, maximum.\n2. text-wrap: balance distributes lines.\n3. line-height tight for display.
accessibility_across_viewports: Real h1. Balance prevents orphans.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Linear vw scales too small at narrow.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Aggressive clamp. 2) Balance prevents orphans. 3) Tight letter-spacing for display.

title: Margin between sections that scales with section size, not viewport
scenario_context: Page with hero, features, testimonials. Margin between sections should be proportional to section content size, not just viewport.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Hero is huge — generous margin after.\n2. Footer is compact — less margin.\n3. Per-section margins.
accessibility_across_viewports: Sections logical landmarks.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Same padding everywhere = some sections cramped, others sparse.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Per-section scale. 2) Fluid within scale. 3) Visual rhythm matches content.

title: Touch swipe gestures with velocity tracking for natural momentum
scenario_context: Swipable card stack (Tinder-style). Velocity determines whether to snap to next/prev or back. Mouse drag works with same logic.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Distance-only check (slow drag past threshold = unwanted action)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Velocity makes gestures feel native.\n2. Track samples for smooth velocity.\n3. Pointer events unify.
performance_notes: Sample array capped at 10. Velocity calc O(1).
accessibility_across_viewports: Touch gestures should have keyboard alternative.
edge_cases_handled: Slow drag past threshold — distance commits
what_breaks_naive_approach: Distance-only is unnatural; users flick expecting fast commit.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Velocity + distance. 2) Recent samples. 3) Universal physics.

title: Drawer that auto-resizes to fit keyboard when input focused inside
scenario_context: Side drawer with form. When input focused, keyboard appears. Drawer must adjust so input stays visible.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Drawer at fixed height with form behind keyboard
responsive_reasoning: 1. Drawer adjusts to visualViewport height when keyboard summoned.\n2. Maintains state across keyboard transitions.\n3. Internal scroll if needed.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Form in drawer, keyboard appears, drawer adjusts

accessibility_across_viewports: role=dialog aria-modal. Focus stays in drawer.
edge_cases_handled: Form input focused — drawer shrinks above keyboard
Keyboard dismissed — drawer back to full
what_breaks_naive_approach: Drawer at static height has form fields behind keyboard.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Keyboard-aware height. 2) Smooth transitions. 3) Internal scroll if needed.

title: Bottom action bar with primary CTA that respects multiple bottom UI elements
viewport_spectrum: Apps with bottom nav + safe areas + keyboard
scenario_context: App has bottom nav + bottom action bar (e.g., 'Save' button on profile edit). Action bar must clear all bottom UI plus safe area.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Multiple bottom elements stack.\n2. CSS variables for each element height.\n3. Calc adds them up.

detection_strategy.what_to_detect: Bottom nav presence + safe area + keyboard

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Action bar above all bottom UI

performance_notes: Pure CSS with single var lookup.
accessibility_across_viewports: Sticky preserves focus.
edge_cases_handled: Bottom nav + action bar — action bar above nav
Keyboard appears — action bar floats higher
Desktop — none of these, baseline behavior
Multiple stacking elements — all accounted via vars
what_breaks_naive_approach: Hardcoded bottom: 56px conflicts when nav changes height or device has indicator.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) CSS var stacking. 2) Keyboard + safe area. 3) Adaptive composition.

title: Drawer that becomes side panel in landscape phone for better access
scenario_context: Settings drawer. Portrait: full-width from bottom. Landscape phone: side panel because vertical space is precious.
responsive_reasoning: 1. Portrait drawer from bottom natural.\n2. Landscape side panel preserves vertical.\n3. Orientation switches presentation.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Portrait: swipe up. Landscape: swipe from side.

accessibility_across_viewports: role=dialog aria-modal preserved.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Bottom drawer in landscape phone eats vertical space.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Orientation drives position. 2) Safe area aware. 3) Same content.

title: Reading mode on tablet that adapts column count to comfortable line length
scenario_context: E-reader on iPad. Portrait: single wide column. Landscape: two columns side-by-side (book metaphor).
responsive_reasoning: 1. iPad landscape has horizontal room for 2 columns.\n2. Portrait better as single 65ch column.\n3. Same content reflows.
accessibility_across_viewports: Reading order preserved (column flow respects DOM).
edge_cases_handled: Heading inside columns — break-after-avoid keeps with paragraph
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single column landscape iPad has lines too long for comfortable reading.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Column count per orientation. 2) Natural CSS columns. 3) Reading order preserved.

title: Trello-style kanban board with horizontal scroll columns and vertical scroll cards
scenario_context: Kanban board with columns. Mobile: horizontal scroll between columns, each column vertically scrollable. Desktop: all columns visible.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Vertical stacking of columns on mobile (loses kanban metaphor)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Kanban is inherently horizontal.\n2. Mobile horizontal scroll preserves model.\n3. Each column scrolls vertically independently.

detection_strategy.code_pattern: overflow-x: auto; > col { flex: none; width: 280px; }

layout_strategy.information_density_changes: Mobile shows one column at a time; desktop all

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Swipe between columns, scroll cards in column
interaction_model_per_device.desktop_mouse: All visible, scroll cards in column

performance_notes: Native scroll-snap. Each column independent scroll context.
accessibility_across_viewports: Real headers per column. Cards as list items.
edge_cases_handled: Long card lists — vertical scroll within column
what_breaks_naive_approach: Stacking columns vertically on mobile destroys kanban metaphor.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Horizontal scroll preserves model. 2) Snap on mobile. 3) Each column independent scroll.

title: Column resize handle that works for both touch and mouse with keyboard accessibility
scenario_context: Resize column widths. Mouse: drag handle. Touch: drag handle (wider). Keyboard: focused header + arrow keys.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Resize handle that conflicts with column click for sort
responsive_reasoning: 1. Resize separate from sort (separate handle).\n2. Handle wider on touch.\n3. Keyboard support via aria-valuenow.

layout_strategy.primary_technique: Separate resize handle adjacent to header label

accessibility_across_viewports: Header is tabbable. role=separator on handle. aria-valuenow. Keyboard resize via Shift+Arrow.
edge_cases_handled: Touch — wider handle. Mouse — narrow. Keyboard — Shift+Arrow
what_breaks_naive_approach: Mouse-only resize locks out touch + keyboard.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Pointer events. 2) Pointer-aware handle width. 3) Keyboard resize.

title: File upload drag-and-drop zone with mobile camera capture button
scenario_context: Upload zone. Desktop: drag-and-drop + browse. Mobile: tap to choose source (camera, library, files).
anti_patterns_to_avoid: No camera capture option (forces file picker)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Native file input supports capture attribute for camera.\n2. accept narrows file types.\n3. Drag-and-drop for desktop power.
accessibility_across_viewports: Labels associate with inputs. sr-only inputs.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Mobile users can't drag — need explicit options.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Mobile source picker. 2) Desktop drag UX. 3) Camera capture support.

title: Logo that swaps between full and icon-only versions per viewport
scenario_context: Brand logo. Wide: full wordmark. Narrow: icon only. CSS swap via picture or hidden classes.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Single SVG that's too wide on mobile
responsive_reasoning: 1. Full logo unreadable when scaled down.\n2. Icon-only on narrow preserves brand.\n3. picture element or CSS visibility.

detection_strategy.ssr_considerations: Both SVGs in DOM, CSS shows one

performance_notes: Browser picks correct version. SVGs are tiny.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-label on link. alt on img.
edge_cases_handled: Dark mode — could use prefers-color-scheme picture sources
what_breaks_naive_approach: Single full logo shrinks unreadable on mobile.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Picture element variants. 2) Native browser selection. 3) Universal brand presence.

title: Search bar that expands from icon on small viewports to inline input on wide
scenario_context: Nav has search. Wide: inline input always visible. Narrow: icon button expands to overlay or fills nav width.
anti_patterns_to_avoid: Tiny inline search on mobile (no room)
responsive_reasoning: 1. Mobile lacks room for both nav + persistent search.\n2. Icon expands to overlay or full width.\n3. Same search results.

interaction_model_per_device.mobile_touch: Tap search icon → input fills nav with auto-focus

performance_notes: Input mounts only when expanded on mobile.
accessibility_across_viewports: aria-label on icon and input. autoFocus on expand.
what_breaks_naive_approach: Tiny inline search on 320px is unusable. Icon-only on desktop wastes space.
what_makes_this_truly_responsive: 1) Expand pattern on mobile. 2) Inline on desktop. 3) AutoFocus on expand.
